revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=菊池神社| 所在地=福岡県福岡市城南区七隈7-10| 祭神=菊池武時命
仁徳天皇
埴安大神| 社格=[[県社]]| 創建=明治2年（1869年）| 例祭=[[4月13日]] }} '''菊池神社'''（きくちじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[城南区]]にある[[神社]]である。 [[鎌倉時代]]末期、[[後醍醐天皇]]による倒幕の魁として博多で玉砕した[[菊池武時]]を祭神とする。旧社格は[[県社]]。 ==歴史== [[元弘]]3年（[[1333年]]）3月11日、[[鎮西探題]]に攻め寄せた菊池武時は[[少弐氏]]・[[大友氏]]の離反によって敗死。馬上の武時は六本松付近で首が落ち、七隈でその胴体が落ちたといわれる。この際に胴体を祭った胴塚が七隈菊池神社の創始とされる。 その後胴塚は戦乱の最中に荒廃しいつしか忘れ去られていったが、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）、菊池氏の末裔である黒田藩士・城武貞の建白により藩主・[[黒田斉清]]の認可を受け墓碑を建立。翌[[天保]]3年には武時没後五百年祭を斎行し藩主の幣帛を賜った。 明治2年（1869年）、藩主・[[黒田長知]]によって墓碑地に社殿が建立。大正8年（1919年）、早良郡内の有志によって七隈の氏神である埴安神社が当地に移転合祀される。大正11年（1922年）3月16日、村社に列し、昭和8年（1933年）4月4日には県社に昇格。さらに4月7日に神饌幣帛料供進指定社となり、4月13日の武時没後六百年大祭が盛大に挙行された。 ==関連項目== *[[菊池氏]] *[[南北朝時代 (日本)]] *[[建武の新政]] {{神道 横}} {{デフォルトソート:きくちしんしや}} [[Category:福岡市の神社]] [[Category:菊池氏]]

{{神社| 名称=菊池神社| 所在地=福岡県福岡市城南区七隈7-10| 祭神=菊池武時命

{{神社| 名称=菊池神社| 画像 = [[File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG|260px]]| 所在地=[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[城南区]][[七隈]]7-10| 祭神=[[菊池武時]]命
[[仁徳天皇]]
埴安大神| 社格=[[県社]]| 創建=[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]）| 例祭=[[4月13日]] }} '''菊池神社'''（きくちじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[城南区]]にある[[神社]]である。 [[鎌倉時代]]末期、[[後醍醐天皇]]による倒幕の魁として博多で玉砕した[[菊池武時]]を祭神とする。旧社格は[[県社]]。 ==歴史== [[元弘]]3年（[[1333年]]）3月11日、[[鎮西探題]]に攻め寄せた菊池武時は[[少弐氏]]・[[大友氏]]の離反によって敗死。馬上の武時は[[六本松]]付近で首が落ち、七隈でその胴体が落ちたといわれる。この際に胴体を祭った胴塚が七隈菊池神社の創始とされる。 その後胴塚は戦乱の最中に荒廃しいつしか忘れ去られていったが、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）、菊池氏の末裔である[[福岡藩]]士・[[城武貞]]の建白により藩主・[[黒田斉清]]の認可を受け墓碑を建立。翌[[天保]]3年には武時没後五百年祭を斎行し藩主の幣帛を賜った。 明治2年（1869年）、藩主・[[黒田長知]]によって墓碑地に社殿が建立。大正8年（1919年）、[[早良郡]]内の有志によって七隈の氏神である埴安神社が当地に移転合祀される。大正11年（1922年）3月16日、村社に列し、昭和8年（1933年）4月4日には県社に昇格。さらに4月7日に神饌幣帛料供進指定社となり、4月13日の武時没後六百年大祭が盛大に挙行された。 ==関連項目== *[[南北朝時代 (日本)]] *[[建武の新政]] *[[菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きくちしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の神社]] [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:城南区の歴史]] [[Category:福岡市の建築物]] [[Category:菊池氏|社]]

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2020年9月}} {{神社 | 名称=菊池神社 | 画像 = [[File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG|260px]] | 所在地=[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[城南区]][[七隈]]7-10 | 祭神=[[菊池武時]]命
[[仁徳天皇]]
埴安大神 | 社格=[[県社]] | 創建=[[明治]]2年（[[1869年]]） | 例祭=[[4月13日]] }} '''菊池神社'''（きくちじんじゃ）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[城南区]]にある[[神社]]である。 [[鎌倉時代]]末期、[[後醍醐天皇]]による倒幕の魁として博多で玉砕した[[菊池武時]]を祭神とする。旧社格は[[県社]]。 ==歴史== [[元弘]]3年（[[1333年]]）3月11日、[[鎮西探題]]に攻め寄せた菊池武時は[[少弐氏]]・[[大友氏]]の離反によって敗死。馬上の武時は[[六本松]]付近で首が落ち、七隈でその胴体が落ちたといわれる。この際に胴体を祭った胴塚が七隈菊池神社の創始とされる。 その後胴塚は戦乱の最中に荒廃しいつしか忘れ去られていったが、[[天保]]2年（[[1831年]]）、菊池氏の末裔である[[福岡藩]]士・[[城武貞]]の建白により藩主・[[黒田斉清]]の認可を受け墓碑を建立。翌[[天保]]3年には武時没後五百年祭を斎行し藩主の幣帛を賜った。 明治2年（1869年）、藩主・[[黒田長知]]によって墓碑地に社殿が建立。大正8年（1919年）、[[早良郡]]内の有志によって七隈の氏神である埴安神社が当地に移転合祀される。大正11年（1922年）3月16日、村社に列し、昭和8年（1933年）4月4日には県社に昇格。さらに4月7日に神饌幣帛料供進指定社となり、4月13日の武時没後六百年大祭が盛大に挙行された。 ==関連項目== *[[南北朝時代 (日本)]] *[[建武の新政]] *[[菊池神社 (菊池市)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:きくちしんしや}} [[Category:福岡県の旧県社]] [[Category:福岡市の神社]] [[Category:城南区の歴史]] [[Category:城南区の建築物]] [[Category:菊池氏|社]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages needing history import]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
{{shrine | name = Kikuchi Shrine | image = [[File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG|260px]] | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}} 7-10 | deities = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=}} no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|qq=}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|qq=}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|qq=}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|qq=}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|qq=}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]]{{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:城南区の建築物
revision.text:
{{shrine | name = Kikuchi Shrine | image = [[File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG|260px]] | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}} 7-10 | deities = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=}} no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|qq=}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|qq=}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|qq=}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|qq=}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|qq=}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]]{{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:城南区の建築物]]

{{shrine | name = Kikuchi Shrine | image = [[File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG|260px]] | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}} 7-10 | deities = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=}} no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|qq=}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|qq=}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|qq=}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|qq=}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|qq=}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]]{{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:城南区の建築物]] [[Category:城南区の歴史]] [[Category:福岡市の神社]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:菊池氏]]

{{shrine | name = Kikuchi Shrine | image = [[File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG|260px]] | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}} 7-10 | deities = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=}} no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|qq=}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|qq=}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|qq=}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|qq=}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|qq=}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[en:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]]{{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:城南区の歴史]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}}

On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]]{{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:城南区の歴史]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}}

revision.text:
{{shrine | name = Kikuchi Shrine | image = [[File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG|260px]] | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}} 7-10 | deities = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|qq=}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|qq=}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|qq=}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]]{{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:城南区の歴史]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}}

The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|qq=}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Japan]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]]{{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:城南区の歴史]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}}

The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|qq=}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]]{{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:城南区の歴史]] [[Category:Shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11619835]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{shrine | name = Kikuchi Shrine | image = [[File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG|260px]] | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}} 7-10 | deities = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|qq=}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|qq=}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|qq=}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]]{{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}}

It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|qq=}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|qq=}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|qq=}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|lt=Son-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Village_shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#Metropolitan_and_Prefectural_Shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] [[sn:Kikuchi Shrine (Fukuoka)]]{{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}}

{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] | location = {{ill|Jōnan-ku|qid=Q948351}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.551268|N|130.360783|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|qid=Q94316}} }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|qq=|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|qq=|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|qq=|1=Kuroda Nariyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}}

[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] | location = {{ill|Jōnan-ku|WD=Q948351}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.551268|N|130.360783|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|qq=|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|qq=|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|qq=|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|qq=|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|qq=|1=Kuroda Nariyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11619835]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11619835]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine | image = File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}} 7-10 | deities = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] | location = {{ill|Jōnan-ku|WD=Q948351}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.551268|N|130.360783|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Nariyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11619835]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11619835]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine | image = File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}} 7-10 | deities = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] | location = {{ill|Jōnan-ku|WD=Q948351}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.551268|N|130.360783|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Nariyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[sn:commons:Category:Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11619835]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11619835]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine | image = File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}} 7-10 | deities = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] | location = {{ill|Jōnan-ku|WD=Q948351}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.551268|N|130.360783|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Nariyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
