[[commons:Category:Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[commons:Category:Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11619835]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11619835]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine | image = File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}} 7-10 | deities = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] | location = {{ill|Jōnan-ku|WD=Q948351}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.551268|N|130.360783|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Nariyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Nariyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

[[commons:Category:Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[commons:Category:Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11619835]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:31, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11619835]] Wikidata name: Kikuchi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:11, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine | image = File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG | location = [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}} 7-10 | deities = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} no Mikoto
[[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]]
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]) | annual_festival = [[:en:April 13|April 13]] | location = {{ill|Jōnan-ku|WD=Q948351}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.551268|N|130.360783|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|Jōnan-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka|Fukuoka]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under [[:en:Emperor Go-Daigo|Emperor Go-Daigo]], died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō era]] 2 ([[:en:1831|1831]]) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the [[:en:Fukuoka Domain|Fukuoka Domain]] and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Nariyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. In [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 8 ([[:en:1919|1919]]), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, [[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 11 ([[:en:1922|1922]]), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, [[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 8 ([[:en:1933|1933]]), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * [[:en:Nanboku-chō period|Nanboku-chō period]] * [[:en:Kenmu Restoration|Kenmu Restoration]] * [[:en:Kikuchi Shrine|Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)]] {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In [[:en:Tenpō|Tenpō]] 3 ([[:en:1832|1832]]), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 2 ([[:en:1869|1869]]), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord [[:en:Kuroda Nagatomo|Kuroda Nagachika]]. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}, and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō era}} 2 ({{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831}}) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain}} and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Nariyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 3 ({{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832}}), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagatomo|en|Kuroda Nagatomo|WD=Q6446276|lt=Kuroda Nagachika}}. In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 8 ({{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}}), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 11 ({{ill|1922|en|1922|WD=Q2165|lt=1922}}), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 8 ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933}}), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}} * {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration}} * {{ill|Kikuchi Shrine|en|Kikuchi Shrine|WD=Q94317|lt=Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 8 ({{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}}), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 11 ({{ill|1922|en|1922|WD=Q2165|lt=1922}}), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 8 ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933}}), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}} * {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration}} * {{ill|Kikuchi Shrine|en|Kikuchi Shrine|WD=Q94317|lt=Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[sn:commons:Category:Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)]][[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] {{Infobox religious building | name = Kikuchi Shrine | image = File:Nanakumakikuchi.JPG | location = {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|en|Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q948351|lt=Jōnan-ku}}, {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}} 7-10 | deities = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} no Mikoto
{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}) | annual_festival = {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13}} | location = {{ill|Jōnan-ku|WD=Q948351}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.551268|N|130.360783|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|en|Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q948351|lt=Jōnan-ku}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}, and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō era}} 2 ({{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831}}) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain}} and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Nariyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 3 ({{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832}}), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagatomo|en|Kuroda Nagatomo|WD=Q6446276|lt=Kuroda Nagachika}}. In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 8 ({{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}}), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 11 ({{ill|1922|en|1922|WD=Q2165|lt=1922}}), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 8 ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933}}), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}} * {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration}} * {{ill|Kikuchi Shrine|en|Kikuchi Shrine|WD=Q94317|lt=Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}) | annual_festival = {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13}} | location = {{ill|Jōnan-ku|WD=Q948351}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.551268|N|130.360783|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}} }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|en|Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q948351|lt=Jōnan-ku}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}, and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō era}} 2 ({{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831}}) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain}} and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Nariyuki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō}} 3 ({{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832}}), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869}}), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagatomo|en|Kuroda Nagatomo|WD=Q6446276|lt=Kuroda Nagachika}}. In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 8 ({{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919}}), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 11 ({{ill|1922|en|1922|WD=Q2165|lt=1922}}), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 8 ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933}}), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period}} * {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration}} * {{ill|Kikuchi Shrine|en|Kikuchi Shrine|WD=Q94317|lt=Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō era|1=Tenpō}} 2 ({{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831|1=1831}}) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}} and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Narikiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 3 ({{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}}), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagatomo|en|Kuroda Nagatomo|WD=Q6446276|lt=Kuroda Nagachika|1=Kuroda Nagatomo}}. In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 8 ({{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}}), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707|1=Haniyasu Shrine}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|1922|en|1922|WD=Q2165|lt=1922|1=1922}}), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 8 ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933|1=1933}}), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} * {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration|1=Kenmu Restoration}} * {{ill|Kikuchi Shrine|en|Kikuchi Shrine|WD=Q94317|lt=Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)|1=Kikuchi Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 3 ({{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}}), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagatomo|en|Kuroda Nagatomo|WD=Q6446276|lt=Kuroda Nagachika|1=Kuroda Nagatomo}}. In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 8 ({{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}}), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707|1=Haniyasu Shrine}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|1922|en|1922|WD=Q2165|lt=1922|1=1922}}), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 8 ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933|1=1933}}), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} * {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration|1=Kenmu Restoration}} * {{ill|Kikuchi Shrine|en|Kikuchi Shrine|WD=Q94317|lt=Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)|1=Kikuchi Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}) | annual_festival = {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13|1=April 13}} | location = {{ill|Jōnan-ku|WD=Q948351|1=Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.551268|N|130.360783|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316|1=Kikuchi Taketoki}} }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|en|Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q948351|lt=Jōnan-ku|1=Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō era|1=Tenpō}} 2 ({{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831|1=1831}}) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}} and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Narikiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 3 ({{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}}), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagatomo|en|Kuroda Nagatomo|WD=Q6446276|lt=Kuroda Nagachika|1=Kuroda Nagatomo}}. In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 8 ({{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}}), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707|1=Haniyasu Shrine}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|1922|en|1922|WD=Q2165|lt=1922|1=1922}}), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 8 ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933|1=1933}}), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} * {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration|1=Kenmu Restoration}} * {{ill|Kikuchi Shrine|en|Kikuchi Shrine|WD=Q94317|lt=Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)|1=Kikuchi Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} * {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration|1=Kenmu Restoration}} * {{ill|Kikuchi Shrine|en|Kikuchi Shrine|WD=Q94317|lt=Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)|1=Kikuchi Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

{{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}) | annual_festival = {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13|1=April 13}} | location = {{ill|Jōnan-ku|WD=Q948351|1=Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.551268|N|130.360783|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316|1=Kikuchi Taketoki}} }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|en|Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q948351|lt=Jōnan-ku|1=Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō era|1=Tenpō}} 2 ({{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831|1=1831}}) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}} and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Narikiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 3 ({{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}}), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagatomo|en|Kuroda Nagatomo|WD=Q6446276|lt=Kuroda Nagachika|1=Kuroda Nagatomo}}. In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 8 ({{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}}), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707|1=Haniyasu Shrine}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|1922|en|1922|WD=Q2165|lt=1922|1=1922}}), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 8 ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933|1=1933}}), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} * {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration|1=Kenmu Restoration}} * {{ill|Kikuchi Shrine|en|Kikuchi Shrine|WD=Q94317|lt=Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)|1=Kikuchi Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. On March 16, {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|1922|en|1922|WD=Q2165|lt=1922|1=1922}}), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 8 ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933|1=1933}}), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}
{{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | Rank = Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} | founded = {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}) | annual_festival = {{ill|April 13|en|April 13|WD=Q2498|lt=April 13|1=April 13}} | location = {{ill|Jōnan-ku|WD=Q948351|1=Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.551268|N|130.360783|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316|1=Kikuchi Taketoki}} }} '''Kikuchi Shrine''' (菊池神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|en|Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka|WD=Q948351|lt=Jōnan-ku|1=Jōnan-ku, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka|en|Fukuoka|WD=Q26600|lt=Fukuoka|1=Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|WD=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}. It enshrines {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, and {{ill|Haniyasu Ōkami|ja|埴安大神|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|lt=Haniyasu Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Ōkami|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. The shrine held the Rank of a former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. == History == In the late {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|WD=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, {{ill|Kikuchi Taketoki|ja|菊池武時|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kikuchi Taketoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Kikuchi Taketoki|WD=Q94316}}, who led the movement to overthrow the shogunate under {{ill|Emperor Go-Daigo|en|Emperor Go-Daigo|WD=Q313942|lt=Emperor Go-Daigo|1=Emperor Go-Daigo}}, died in battle in {{ill|Hakata|ja|博多|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakata|1=Hakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11284628}}. It is said that his head fell near {{ill|Ropponmatsu|ja|六本松|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ropponmatsu|1=Ropponmatsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392404}} and his torso at {{ill|Nanakuma|ja|七隈|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nanakuma|1=Nanakuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353727}}. A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō era|1=Tenpō}} 2 ({{ill|1831|en|1831|WD=Q7579|lt=1831|1=1831}}) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|WD=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}} and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Narikiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|WD=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 3 ({{ill|1832|en|1832|WD=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}}), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|WD=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagatomo|en|Kuroda Nagatomo|WD=Q6446276|lt=Kuroda Nagachika|1=Kuroda Nagatomo}}. In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 8 ({{ill|1919|en|1919|WD=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}}), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|WD=Q135579707|1=Haniyasu Shrine}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|1922|en|1922|WD=Q2165|lt=1922|1=1922}}), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|WD=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}, and on April 4, {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 8 ({{ill|1933|en|1933|WD=Q18726|lt=1933|1=1933}}), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|WD=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} * {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|WD=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration|1=Kenmu Restoration}} * {{ill|Kikuchi Shrine|en|Kikuchi Shrine|WD=Q94317|lt=Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)|1=Kikuchi Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

A Kofun (dōzuka) was established at the site of his torso, marking the origin of Kikuchi Shrine. The Kofun fell into neglect during subsequent wars but was rediscovered in {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō era|1=Tenpō}} 2 ({{ill|1831|en|1831|qid=Q7579|lt=1831|1=1831}}) by {{ill|Jō Takasada|ja|城武貞|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jō Takasada|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, a samurai of the {{ill|Fukuoka Domain|en|Fukuoka Domain|qid=Q208766|lt=Fukuoka Domain|1=Fukuoka Domain}} and a descendant of the Kikuchi clan. He received permission from the domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nariyuki|ja|黒田斉清|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuroda Nariyuki|1=Kuroda Narikiyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11678605}} to erect a gravestone. In {{ill|Tenpō|en|Tenpō|qid=Q1369045|lt=Tenpō|1=Tenpō}} 3 ({{ill|1832|en|1832|qid=Q7584|lt=1832|1=1832}}), the 500th anniversary of Taketoki's death was commemorated with offerings from the domain lord. In {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 2 ({{ill|1869|en|1869|qid=Q7720|lt=1869|1=1869}}), a shrine building was constructed on the site by domain lord {{ill|Kuroda Nagatomo|en|Kuroda Nagatomo|qid=Q6446276|lt=Kuroda Nagachika|1=Kuroda Nagatomo}}. In {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 8 ({{ill|1919|en|1919|qid=Q2157|lt=1919|1=1919}}), the {{ill|Haniyasu Shrine|ja|埴安神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haniyasu Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579707|1=Haniyasu Shrine}}, the local tutelary shrine of Nanakuma, was relocated and merged here. On March 16, {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 11 ({{ill|1922|en|1922|qid=Q2165|lt=1922|1=1922}}), Kikuchi Shrine was officially designated as a {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}, and on April 4, {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 8 ({{ill|1933|en|1933|qid=Q18726|lt=1933|1=1933}}), it was promoted to the status of a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}. On April 7, it was designated as a shrine receiving government offerings, and on April 13, the grand 600th anniversary festival of Taketoki's death was held. == Related Pages == * {{ill|Nanboku-chō period|en|Nanboku-chō period|qid=Q264012|lt=Nanboku-chō period|1=Nanboku-chō period}} * {{ill|Kenmu Restoration|en|Kenmu Restoration|qid=Q826021|lt=Kenmu Restoration|1=Kenmu Restoration}} * {{ill|Kikuchi Shrine|en|Kikuchi Shrine|qid=Q94317|lt=Kikuchi Shrine (Kikuchi City)|1=Kikuchi Shrine}} {{shinto-stub}} {{citation needed|ja|菊池神社|version=84297807|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] }} [[ja:菊池神社]] [[ja:菊池神社 (福岡市)]] [[ja:%E8%8F%8A%E6%B1%A0%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE (%E7%A6%8F%E5%B2%A1%E5%B8%82)]] {{translated page|ja|菊池神社 (福岡市)|version=79471536|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Kikuchi-jinja (Fukuoka)}} {{wikidata link|Q11619835}} [[Category:Buildings and structures in Jonan-ku, Fukuoka]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Fukuoka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Jonan Ward]] [[Category:Kikuchi clan]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Fukuoka]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
